name: GW polarization is dominantly tensor (+, x)
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict that gravitational waves carry primarily the two transverse-traceless tensor polarizations (+ and x) of GR, not pure scalar (breathing or longitudinal) or pure vector modes. The Bayesian comparison from GW170814 (a three-detector event) strongly favors pure tensor over pure scalar (Bayes factor > 200) and pure vector (Bayes factor > 1000).

regime.description: GW propagation from binary black hole mergers at hundreds of Mpc.

current_bound.value: Bayes factor > 200 favoring pure tensor over pure scalar; > 1000 over pure vector
current_bound.reference: Abbott et al. (LVC), 'GW170814: A Three-Detector Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Coalescence', PRL 119, 141101 (2017), arXiv:1709.09660

what_it_excludes: Pure scalar-tensor theories (Brans-Dicke alone) predict significant scalar GW component and are excluded. Mixed theories must keep non-tensor modes subdominant. With more multi-detector events, GWTC-3 places quantitative bounds on each polarization mode separately.

parameterization.name: GR predicts 2 polarizations (+, x). General metric theories admit up to 6: tensor (+, x), vector (x, y), scalar (breathing, longitudinal). Eardley-Lee-Lightman E(2) classification.
parameterization.reference: Eardley, Lee, Lightman, PRL 30, 884 (1973); Will, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014).
parameterization.summary: Six possible polarization modes; GR populates only two. Multi-detector networks measure the relative amplitude of each.

procedural.predicted_observable: Decomposition of the strain h_ij into + , x, x, y, breathing, longitudinal components, measured as relative amplitudes in a multi-detector network.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Pure tensor strongly favored. Mixed-mode bounds tighten with each new event.
framework_inputs: Compute the polarization content of GW emitted by a generic compact-binary inspiral in the framework. Confront the predicted mode decomposition with multi-detector data.

primary_references.citation: Abbott et al. (LVC), 'GW170814: A Three-Detector Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Coalescence', PRL 119, 141101 (2017)
Eardley, Lee, Lightman, 'Gravitational-Wave Observations as a Tool for Testing Relativistic Gravity', PRL 30, 884 (1973)
